title: Chief People Officer
description: Lead the people function for a clinical-stage biotech scaling toward pivotal trials. Own talent strategy, culture, and leadership development, with a mandate to build an environment where scientific candor is safe and expected.

title: Director of Organizational Development
description: Design and lead organizational development programs across a multi-site manufacturer. Partner with plant leadership on change management, frontline manager capability, and building a durable speak-up culture.

title: Head of Learning & Development
description: Own the enterprise L&D roadmap for a regional financial services firm. Build manager development programs, coaching pathways, and measurement approaches that connect learning to real behavior change.

title: Executive Coach
description: Join a collective of credentialed coaches serving mid-market and enterprise leaders. PCC or MCC credential required, along with experience using validated assessments to support leadership growth.

title: HR Business Partner
description: Partner with clinical and operational leaders across a nonprofit health system. Coach managers, support speak-up and patient safety culture, and translate people data into practical action.

title: Leadership Development Manager
description: Design and facilitate cohort-based leadership programs for emerging and senior leaders. Blend evidence-based content with hands-on practice and measurable outcomes.

title: Industrial-Organizational Psychologist
description: Apply organizational psychology research to real workplace problems. Design studies on team dynamics and psychological safety, and translate findings into tools leaders can use.

title: People & Culture Lead
location: Salt Lake City, Utah, United States
description: Shape people and culture for fast-scaling client organizations. Build onboarding, manager enablement, and feedback practices that hold up as headcount grows.

title: Senior Consultant, Team Effectiveness
description: Advise leadership teams on effectiveness, trust, and decision-making. Run diagnostics, facilitate offsites, and coach leaders through the messy middle of change.

title: Talent Development Partner
description: Support high-growth clients on hiring, onboarding, and manager development. Bring a practical, evidence-informed approach to building capable, candid teams.

title: VP People
description: Lead the people function for an industrial manufacturer. Set HR strategy, elevate frontline leadership, and build a culture where problems surface early rather than late.

title: Organizational Psychology Consultant
description: Deliver organizational development engagements focused on team dynamics, assessment, and change. Strong grounding in applied psychology and comfort in front of executives required.

title: Manager, Leadership & Culture
description: Own leadership and culture initiatives within a financial services people team. Use engagement and people data to target where trust and candor need the most work.

title: Coaching & Development Specialist
description: Deliver coaching and development programs for client managers and emerging leaders. Blend one-on-one coaching with group facilitation and clear measurement.

title: Director of People & Culture
description: Lead people and culture strategy for a health system, with emphasis on clinician well-being, manager capability, and a culture where staff feel safe to raise concerns.

title: Senior Talent Management Consultant
description: Advise clients on leadership assessment, succession, and organizational effectiveness. Comfort with validated assessment tools and executive stakeholders is essential.

title: People Analytics Lead
description: Build the people analytics function for a biotech, including measures of psychological safety and team health. Turn survey and behavioral data into decisions leaders can act on.

title: Program Manager, Manager Enablement
description: Run programs that make new and experienced managers more effective. Focus on the everyday habits that build trust and keep teams talking when the pressure is on.

title: Faculty, Organizational Psychology
description: Teach and research in a graduate organizational and industrial psychology program. Mentor practitioner-scholars and contribute to research on teams, leadership, and workplace behavior.

title: Head of People Development
location: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
description: Lead professional development offerings for a workplace psychology association. Curate content, build community programs, and connect members to practical, research-backed practice.

title: Senior Executive Coach
description: Coach senior leaders in technology and healthcare through transitions and growth. PCC credential and a track record with C-suite clients expected.

title: Learning Experience Designer, Leadership
description: Design engaging learning experiences for leadership programs. Translate research on teams and psychological safety into practical exercises leaders remember and use.
